summary: Research record for hiding username information during sign-in. The app writes DontDisplayUserName, and the local Windows security metadata in sceregvl.inf confirms that exact registry surface for the documented security option.

setting.name: Display of Username During Sign-In
setting.casual_explanation: After someone enters their credentials, Windows can keep showing the username or hide it. This setting decides whether the username stays visible during that sign-in flow.
setting.why_people_change_it: People hide the username at sign-in to reduce on-screen identity exposure in shared spaces or environments where shoulder surfing is a concern.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Hiding the username improves privacy during sign-in, but it also removes a small confirmation cue that can help users notice which account is being used.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft documents this as the security option 'Interactive logon: Do not display username at sign-in'. The current app writes HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\DontDisplayUserName = 1, and local Windows security metadata in sceregvl.inf confirms that exact value name for the documented option. The nearby DontDisplayLockedUserId entry is a separate security-option surface and does not change this mapping.

allowed_values.label: Keep username visible during sign-in
allowed_values.meaning: The security option is not enabled, so the username can remain visible during the sign-in flow.

allowed_values.label: Hide username during sign-in
allowed_values.meaning: The security option is enabled and the username is not displayed after credentials are entered.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes DontDisplayUserName = 1, and the guest-side probe on Win25H2Clean confirmed that sceregvl.inf maps the documented security option to DontDisplayUserName. The earlier DontDisplayLockedUserId interpretation was a different nearby security option entry, not the surface used by this tweak.

windows_defaults.label: Windows security-option default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows client systems exposing the interactive logon security option

states.rationale: The local Windows security baseline files list a default of 0.

windows_defaults.notes: By default, Windows does not force the username to be hidden during sign-in.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Users who want normal sign-in confirmation cues

states.rationale: Keep the normal identity cue visible during sign-in.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want the username to remain visible during sign-in.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want less identity information shown on screen while signing in.
tradeoffs: Users get a clearer confirmation of which account is signing in.
The sign-in screen exposes more identity information.

recommended_profiles.label: Reduced shoulder surfing
avoid_for: Users who prefer more visible sign-in confirmation

states.rationale: Hide the username during the sign-in process.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want less account information visible while credentials are being entered.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want the username to stay visible as a confirmation cue.
tradeoffs: Identity exposure during sign-in is reduced.
Users lose a visible reminder of which account is active in the sign-in flow.

evidence.title: Microsoft security policy setting: Interactive logon: Do not display username at sign-in
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents the purpose of the security option and its enabled and disabled behavior during sign-in.

evidence.title: Local Windows security option registry mapping
evidence.summary: The local Windows security registry metadata maps the security option to HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\DontDisplayUserName.

evidence.title: Local Windows default security baseline entry
evidence.summary: The local Windows default security baseline lists DontDisplayUserName with default value 0.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes DontDisplayUserName = 1 for the current tweak.

evidence.title: Existing tweak source record
evidence.summary: The repo already tracks provenance for this tweak in the current catalog output.

decision.why: Microsoft documents the sign-in privacy behavior and the guest-side VM probe confirmed that sceregvl.inf exposes DontDisplayUserName as the registry surface for this option. The app already writes that exact value, so the record is now validated.

validation_proof.notes: Guest-side probe on Win25H2Clean confirmed that sceregvl.inf contains the DontDisplayUserName mapping for the documented sign-in privacy option. DontDisplayLockedUserId also appears in the same file, but it is a separate nearby security-option surface.
